one_time_description: Large FNFA loan of $37,473,296 at 1.9% maturing June 2030, with corresponding receivable from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group of $35,711,914 — significantly inflated balance sheet in 2021
economic_notes: Operations include tobacco fund, fisheries, gas bar, VLT gaming, and Bayside Development/Operations. COVID-19 caused temporary closure of some commercial operations. Nation has a $50,000 operating line of credit with Bank of Nova Scotia (unused). Group RRSP contributions of $85,360 made during the year.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021. Large FNFA loan of ~$37.5M taken on during the year (1.9% maturing June 2030), with a matching receivable from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group of $35,711,914 — this dramatically inflates both assets and liabilities. Total long-term debt jumped from $12.7M to $52.6M. No schedule of remuneration was included in the provided financial statement text. isc_funding set to 0 as ISC revenue is not broken out as a separate line in the income statement (ISC deferred revenue of $2,102,360 noted on balance sheet; ISC receivable of $520,000 in Note 2). total_government_transfers estimated from identifiable government revenue lines: Education ($1,341,415) + Social services ($2,444,704) + Capital ($1,140,109) + Operations and maintenance ($333,596) + Economic development ($556,076) + First Nation financial management ($1,918,393) + Health Canada ($1,588,043) + CMHC housing lines ($9,577 + $279,205) + Non-subsidized housing ($190,582) - some lines may include own-source. Housing expenses combine CMHC and non-subsidized housing expenditure lines. expenses_infrastructure combines Capital ($544,337) and Operations and maintenance ($208,045). expenses_enterprises combines Tobacco, Fisheries, Gas Bar, VLT, Bayside Development and Bayside Operations expenditures. COVID-19 noted as causing temporary commercial closures.
